task_description: Create a new presentation, add a new slide, and insert a local screenshot image into it.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a semitransparent red X centered on the Windows Start button in the taskbar. The button is a rounded square containing the blue four-pane Windows logo, positioned immediately to the left of the search box labeled in Chinese with a magnifying-glass icon and the text “搜索”. In the full-screen view, the desktop is visible with many application shortcuts and folders, and the taskbar is centered at the bottom; the target is clearly a taskbar control in the bottom area, not a desktop icon or blank region.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the Windows Start menu from the taskbar to begin launching or searching for something.
caption.action: Click the Windows Start button on the taskbar.
caption.expectation: The Start menu should open above the taskbar, showing pinned apps, recent items, or a search-focused panel.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the pinned Microsoft PowerPoint tile in the Start menu. The tile has the orange PowerPoint icon with a white 'P' over a circular slide graphic, and the label 'PowerPoint' appears below it. Nearby pinned apps include Excel, 邮件, 计算器, 时钟, and 记事本. In the full-screen view, the Windows Start menu is already open in the center-left of the desktop, showing the pinned apps grid; the target is a UI control inside the Start menu, not a desktop icon or blank area.
caption.think: The user likely wants to launch Microsoft PowerPoint from the Start menu.
caption.action: Click the PowerPoint pinned app tile in the Start menu.
caption.expectation: The PowerPoint tile should register the selection and Microsoft PowerPoint should begin opening, likely replacing focus from the Start menu with the app window or its loading screen.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the large template tile labeled “空白演示文稿” with a plain white slide thumbnail. This is a clickable UI control in PowerPoint’s start screen under the “新建” section, not a canvas object or blank area. In the full-screen view, Microsoft PowerPoint is open to its home/start page, with the left sidebar showing options such as 开始, 新建, and 打开, and the blank presentation template is the first tile in the templates row.
caption.think: The user likely wants to create a new empty PowerPoint file by selecting the Blank Presentation template from the startup screen.
caption.action: Click the “空白演示文稿” (Blank Presentation) template tile.
caption.expectation: The blank template tile should be selected and PowerPoint should open a new empty presentation, switching from the start screen to the editing workspace with a new slide.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X placed on the small floating mini-toolbar/context menu beside the slide area, specifically over the left side near the clipboard-style paste icon under the label “粘贴选项:”. The menu also visibly includes paste-related icons, plus the commands “新建幻灯片(N)” and “新增节(A)”. In the full-screen view, PowerPoint is open with a new blank presentation containing one title slide. The context menu is open in the left portion of the editing workspace near the slide thumbnail pane, indicating the right-click occurred in a blank or pane area rather than on a shape or text object. The menu options are area-management and paste options, not object-formatting commands.
caption.think: The user likely right-clicked in the left slide thumbnail/navigation area or nearby blank workspace to inspect the context menu. Since the open menu contains paste options, new slide, and add section commands, this is a context menu for slide-pane management rather than for editing a selected object on the slide.
caption.action: Right-click in the blank area near the slide thumbnail pane / left workspace area to open the same context menu.
caption.expectation: A context menu should appear with paste-related options and slide-management commands such as creating a new slide and adding a section, confirming the click was on a blank pane area rather than on a slide object.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the context-menu command labeled “新建幻灯片(N)” with a small slide thumbnail icon. This is a menu item in a floating right-click menu, so the target is a UI control rather than a canvas object, blank region, or handle. In the full-screen view, PowerPoint is open with a single blank title slide, and the same context menu is visible beside the left slide thumbnail pane, containing paste options, “新建幻灯片(N),” and “新增节(A).”
caption.think: The user likely wants to insert another slide into the presentation from the slide pane context menu.
caption.action: Click the “新建幻灯片(N)” menu item.
caption.expectation: The context menu should close and a new slide should be added to the presentation, appearing in the left thumbnail pane as an additional slide.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the ribbon tab labeled “插入” at the top of PowerPoint. This is a top toolbar/ribbon UI control, not an object on the slide canvas. In the full-screen view, PowerPoint is open with two slide thumbnails on the left, the second slide selected, and a title-and-content layout visible in the main editing area. The ribbon is currently on the “开始” tab, so clicking “插入” would switch the ribbon to the Insert tools.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the Insert tab so they can add content such as pictures, shapes, charts, or other objects to the selected slide.
caption.expectation: The ribbon should switch from the Home tab to the Insert tab, updating the top toolbar to show insert-related commands.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the large ribbon button labeled “图片,” which has a small landscape-style picture icon with mountains and a sun. This is a top ribbon UI control in PowerPoint’s “插入” tab, specifically in the “图像” group, not an object on the slide canvas. In the full-screen view, PowerPoint is open with slide 2 selected in the left thumbnail pane, and the main slide uses a title-and-content layout. The Insert ribbon is already active, showing image-related options such as “图片,” “屏幕截图,” and “相册.”
caption.think: The user likely wants to insert an image onto the currently selected slide by opening the picture insertion workflow from the Insert tab.
caption.action: Click the “图片” button on the Insert ribbon.
caption.expectation: PowerPoint should open a picture insertion chooser or file selection dialog, allowing the user to browse for and insert an image into the current slide.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the dropdown menu item labeled “此设备(D)...” under the header “插入图片来自.” This is a UI control in a popup menu opened from the “图片” button on the Insert ribbon; the item has a small picture-and-monitor style icon and is the first option in the menu. In the full-screen view, PowerPoint is open with slide 2 selected, the “插入” tab active, and the picture dropdown menu expanded above the slide canvas.
caption.think: The user likely wants to insert an image from the current computer into the selected slide by opening the local file picker.
caption.expectation: The dropdown menu should close and a file selection dialog should open for choosing an image from this device.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a file thumbnail labeled “屏幕截图.” The thumbnail is a yellow folder-style image preview with a small screenshot visible on it, and it is highlighted with a blue selection border inside the file picker. In the full-screen view, a Windows “插入图片” file selection dialog is open over PowerPoint, with the Pictures library displayed and several image files and folders visible. The selected item “屏幕截图” is in the upper-right area of the file list, and the “打开(O)” button is available at the bottom right of the dialog.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open this selected image file and insert it into the current PowerPoint slide. Because this step is a double-click on a graphic file thumbnail in the file picker, the intent is to confirm the selection and insert the image immediately.
caption.action: Double-click the selected image file thumbnail labeled “屏幕截图.”
caption.expectation: The file dialog should close, and the chosen image should be inserted onto the current PowerPoint slide.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the selected image thumbnail labeled “屏幕截图 2025-03-01 154046.png.” The thumbnail preview contains a small grayscale diagram, and the file tile is highlighted with a light blue selection background. This is a file item inside the file picker, not a ribbon control or slide object. In the full-screen view, a Windows “插入图片” dialog is open over PowerPoint, showing multiple screenshot files in the Pictures folder, with this leftmost file already selected and the “打开(O)” button available at the bottom right.
caption.think: The user likely wants to confirm or keep focus on this already selected image file so it can be inserted into the PowerPoint slide. A single click here reinforces the selection rather than opening it immediately.
caption.action: Click the selected image file thumbnail labeled “屏幕截图 2025-03-01 154046.png.”
caption.expectation: The file thumbnail should remain selected in the dialog, with no major window change yet; the image stays highlighted and ready to be opened or inserted.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue button labeled “插入(S)” with a small downward arrow attached on its right side. This is a dialog button control at the bottom of the file picker, not a canvas object. In the full-screen view, a Windows “插入图片” dialog is open over PowerPoint, a screenshot file named “屏幕截图 2025-03-01 154046.png” is selected in the file list, and the dialog is ready to confirm insertion.
caption.think: The user has already selected the desired image file and now wants to confirm the choice so the file is inserted into the current PowerPoint slide.
caption.expectation: The file dialog should close, and the selected image should be inserted onto the current PowerPoint slide.
